HTML / CSS

Build responsive web UIs from scratch

Semantic HTML5, Flexbox, Grid, animations, Arabic RTL layouts, accessibility, and a live split-pane preview window for every exercise.

API Development

Build, secure and deploy production REST APIs

Express, PostgreSQL, JWT auth, ZATCA Phase 2 integration, Redis caching, payment gateways, testing, OpenAPI docs, and a full BSS API capstone.

28 Lessons8 Chapters84 Training HoursNode.js · REST · ZATCA
🚀

Production Tips

Deploy, monitor, debug and secure live applications

Server setup, Nginx/SSL, Docker, CI/CD, zero-downtime deploy, monitoring, APM, log analysis, profiling, OWASP security, DDoS protection, backups, and go-live playbook.

30 Lessons9 Chapters90 Training HoursLinux · Docker · Security

Flutter

Master cross-platform mobile development

Dart, State Management, UI components, ZATCA integration, Gedia/STC Pay, security, CI/CD and App Store deployment.

iOS / Swift

Native iOS apps with SwiftUI

Swift, SwiftUI, Core Data, networking, ZATCA, Apple Pay, and App Store deployment.

🎨

UI/UX Design

Design systems, Figma mastery, RTL/Arabic UI

Gestalt principles, color theory, typography, wireframing, prototyping, RTL/Arabic design, accessibility, and a BSS admin dashboard capstone.

22 Lessons7 Chapters66 Training HoursFigma · RTL · WCAG
🎨

CSS Mastery

Advanced selectors, layouts, animations

CSS Grid, Flexbox, custom properties, animations, transitions, container queries, and modern responsive patterns with a live split-pane preview.

24 Lessons8 Chapters72 Training HoursCSS · Grid · Flexbox
🇸🇦

ZATCA / E-Invoicing

Saudi Phase 2 e-invoicing compliance

ZATCA Phase 2 clearance, UBL 2.1 XML, CSR generation, cryptographic stamps, QR codes, clearance API, and full BSS POS integration.

24 Lessons8 Chapters72 Training HoursZATCA · XML · KSA
🐍

Advanced Python

Backend mastery with FastAPI & async

Type system, async/await, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Celery task queues, pytest, and production BSS integration patterns for the Saudi market.

20 Lessons8 Chapters60 Training HoursPython · FastAPI · Async
⚡

Advanced JavaScript

Modern JS, TypeScript & performance

V8 internals, TypeScript 5, ES modules, functional programming, React performance optimization, and modern build tooling with Vite.

20 Lessons8 Chapters57 Training HoursJS · TypeScript · React
🐳

Advanced Docker

Containers, orchestration & security

Multi-stage builds, Docker Compose, SAMA security compliance, CI/CD pipelines, image optimization, and Kubernetes migration strategies.

20 Lessons8 Chapters55 Training HoursDocker · K8s · SAMA
📈

SaaS Marketing

Metrics, pricing & go-to-market that pay back

CAC, LTV, churn and NRR; unit economics and margins; pricing and positioning; B2B vs B2C; client management, retention and expansion — worked in SAR for the Saudi market.

Saudi-focused · ZATCA · Gedia/STC Pay · Arabic RTL · App Store ready

Go-To-Market Strategy
PHASE 1 · M1–M3
Anchor Clients
  • Matcha Valley & SDL as live references
  • Free 3-month pilot for 3 EP businesses
  • Eastern Province Chamber via BD network
  • Target: cafés, clinics, retail in Dammam
PHASE 2 · M4–M9
SME Outreach
  • LinkedIn + Snapchat ads targeting EP SMEs
  • ZATCA Phase 2 compliance urgency messaging
  • Referral: SAR 300/successful merchant intro
  • Partner with accounting firms
PHASE 3 · M10–M18
Scale & Expand
  • iOS & Flutter client apps (Kinzhal-built)
  • Enterprise tier SAR 1,800/mo multi-branch
  • Expand to Riyadh & Jeddah via resellers
  • Seek Monsha'at / SIDF growth financing
COMPETITIVE EDGE
Why BSS Wins
  • ZATCA Phase 2 native — not retrofitted
  • Arabic-first UI with RTL support
  • EP-based support — same timezone, Arabic
  • Founder operates BSS daily (Matcha / SDL)
Risk Assessment
HIGH
ZATCA API changes mid-rolloutZATCA has changed Phase 2 specs 3 times. Mitigation: versioned API adapter layer, maintain sandbox parity.
HIGH
Slow SME sales cycleSaudi SMEs average 6–10 weeks to commit. Mitigation: monthly billing, no lock-in, free migration from Foodics/Marn.
MED
Cash burn before BEPEstimated SAR 85,000 in costs before profitability. Mitigation: SDL & Matcha revenue subsidises, or pre-sell annual contracts.
LOW
Technical scalingStack handles 500+ concurrent merchants. Add read replicas at 100 merchants. No re-architecture until 1,000+.
✅
Profitable — Conditionally
BSS is financially viable if it reaches the break-even merchant count within 12 months. With ZATCA compliance urgency as a forcing function and Kinzhal's technical advantage in Arabic-first development, this is achievable. Recommended: focus first 90 days entirely on locking in 5 paying anchor clients.
☕ Matcha Valley — Specialty Coffee Shop · Dammam
📝 Matcha Valley · F&B · Coffee Shop · Al Khobar / Dammam

Go-To-Market Strategy
BRAND
Identity & Positioning
  • Premium specialty coffee — Gen-Z aesthetic
  • Japanese craft + Saudi hospitality culture
  • Valley Warriors collectible toy line (upsell)
  • Instagram-worthy interior — content magnet
LOYALTY ENGINE
Flutter PWA App
  • Spin wheel, points, free drink rewards
  • Push notifications for specials & new blends
  • Bilingual AR/EN interface
  • Repeat visit rate target: 40%+
REVENUE STREAMS
Beyond the Cup
  • Retail whole-bean bags & accessories
  • Valley Warriors mech-samurai collectibles
  • Private cupping session bookings
  • B2B: supply specialty coffee to hotels
SCALING PATH
Year 2 Expansion
  • Second location: Riyadh (Al Nakheel / DQ)
  • Franchise model with BSS POS built-in
  • Online retail via Salla / Zid storefront
  • Seek Kafalah / SIDF for expansion financing
Risk Assessment
HIGH
High rent relative to early volumeRent requires high daily transactions to break even. Mitigation: negotiate 6-month rent-free fit-out, or start in a food hall with lower fixed rent.
MED
Coffee supply chain (specialty beans)Single-origin sourcing adds lead time. Mitigation: maintain 3-month inventory buffer, qualify UAE-based distributor as backup.
MED
Crowded café market in EPKhobar and Dammam have established players. Mitigation: differentiate on specialty quality, collectibles brand, and loyalty app — not price.
LOW
Market sizeEP has 1.2M+ residents. Even 0.1% daily footfall = 1,200+ potential customers. Location selection is the key variable.
✅
Viable — Execution Dependent
Matcha Valley is a specialty coffee shop competing on quality and brand, not price. The loyalty PWA, Valley Warriors collectibles, and BSS POS integration create a defensible moat. Key success factor: location selection in EP — Al Khobar Corniche strip or Dhahran Mall food court as proven F&B footfall zones.

Recommended Launch Sequence
Capital deployment over 18 months
M1–M3: BSS MVP
10K

M8–M12: Matcha
120K

50+ merchants
+45K/mo
Financing Options
BEST
Monsha'at / SIDF SME Loans (0%–5% interest)Government-backed SME financing for Saudi nationals. BSS qualifies as tech startup. Matcha qualifies as F&B SME. Apply for SAR 100K–500K. Requires: business plan, CR, 6 months bank statements.
GOOD
Kafalah Program (Loan Guarantee)Guarantees 50–75% of bank loan for SMEs. Enables SAR 200K+ bank financing for Matcha buildout. Partnered with Al Rajhi, NCB, Riyad Bank. Processing: 4–8 weeks.
ALT
BSS Annual Contract Pre-SalesPre-sell 20 annual BSS contracts at SAR 5,400/yr = SAR 108,000 upfront. Funds Matcha buildout without external debt. Requires 3 anchor pilots first as social proof.
ALT
Revenue-Based Financing (Lean)BSS MRR from month 5 funds marketing. Matcha funded from BSS cash flow by month 8. No dilution, no external debt. Slower but preserves equity.
12-Month P&L Forecast (SAR)
QuarterBSS RevenueF&B RevenueTotal RevenueTotal CostsNet
Q1 (launch phase)4,50004,50035,000-30,500
Q2 (Matcha prep)13,500013,50035,000-21,500
Q3 (Matcha opens)27,000150,000177,000240,000-63,000
Q4 (scaling)54,000252,000306,000240,000+66,000
Year 1 Total99,000438,000537,000620,000-83,000
* Year 1 net loss includes SAR 180K startup capital. Year 2 projects SAR 800K+ net profit at scale.
📈
Year 1 Investment Phase — Year 2+ Highly Profitable
The portfolio requires ~SAR 180K startup capital and operates at a loss in Year 1 while building the three businesses simultaneously. This is normal for an integrated multi-venture launch. Year 2 targets SAR 800K+ annual net profit across the portfolio, with BSS contributing the highest ROI (78% gross margin, pure software). The critical path: BSS scale to 19+ merchants first (pure software, fastest ROI), then Matcha with accumulated capital. Both businesses reinforce each other — BSS powers Matcha operationally, and Matcha is a live BSS reference customer.

Annual billing improves cashflow

🔬 CPU Type Guide — Which to Choose?
SHARED vCPU
Dev & Staging
  • Cost: SAR 15–60/mo (1–2 vCPU)
  • Neighbours share the physical CPU
  • Burst performance — unpredictable
  • ✅ Side projects, staging, <100 users
  • ❌ Never for production databases
DEDICATED vCPU
Production Standard
  • Cost: SAR 80–600/mo (2–16 vCPU)
  • Isolated CPU — consistent performance
  • BSS at 50+ merchants: 2 vCPU dedicated
  • ✅ APIs, web apps, Node.js production
  • ✅ PostgreSQL self-hosted (4+ vCPU)
ARM (Graviton / Ampere)
Best Price/Performance
  • Cost: 20–40% cheaper than x86
  • AWS Graviton3, DO Premium Intel/AMD
  • Node.js runs natively on ARM
  • ✅ Best ROI for SaaS APIs at scale
  • ⚠️ Check Puppeteer/binary compatibility
MEMORY-OPTIMIZED
DB & Cache Heavy
  • Cost: SAR 200–2000/mo (high RAM)
  • High RAM:CPU ratio (8–16 GB per vCPU)
  • PostgreSQL with large working sets
  • ✅ 500+ merchants with complex queries
  • ✅ Redis clusters, analytics workloads
🗄️ Database Selection Guide
DatabaseTypeCost (managed) Best forZATCA / InvoicingBSS Fit
PostgreSQLRelational SQL SAR 80–400/mo Complex queries, ACID transactions, invoicing ✅ Excellent — JSONB for XML ⭐ Primary choice
MySQL / MariaDBRelational SQL SAR 70–350/mo High read throughput, WordPress/PHP legacy ✅ Good — standard SQL ✔ Viable alternative
MongoDB AtlasDocument NoSQL SAR 0–500/mo Flexible schema, rapid iteration, IoT data ⚠️ Manual — no joins for invoice relations ❌ Not ideal for invoicing
RedisIn-memory cache SAR 30–200/mo Session cache, rate limiting, queues ➕ Use alongside PostgreSQL ✅ Add-on, not primary
SQLiteEmbedded file DB Free Single-server dev, small read-heavy apps ⚠️ No concurrent writes, no managed backup ❌ Dev only
Supabase (PG)Managed PostgreSQL SAR 0–95/mo Rapid MVP, built-in auth, realtime ✅ Good — PostgreSQL under hood ✔ Good for BSS MVP phase
📈 Cost Scaling by Merchant Count (BSS Reference)
MerchantsApp ServerDatabase CacheTotal Infra/moCost/MerchantStage

🚀 Deployment Methods — Comparison & Instructions
Which deployment method fits your stage? Costs, complexity, and step-by-step commands for each approach.
✅ RECOMMENDED
Docker + Docker Compose
BSS · Matcha App · Any Node.js SaaS
  • ✅Portable — same image on dev, staging, prod
  • ✅Zero-downtime deploy with health checks
  • ✅PostgreSQL + Redis + Node.js in one file
  • ✅Rollback in seconds (tag previous image)
  • ⚠️Needs 2GB+ RAM on server minimum
Setup cost: SAR 0 · Monthly overhead: 0 · Time: 2–4 hrs
# 1. Build & push image docker build -t bss-api:v1.2 . docker push registry/bss-api:v1.2 # 2. Deploy on server docker compose pull docker compose up -d --no-deps api # 3. Health check docker compose ps curl http://localhost:3000/health
COMMON
PM2 + Bare Metal
Simple VPS · Low memory servers
  • ✅Runs on 512MB RAM servers
  • ✅Auto-restart on crash, log management
  • ✅Cluster mode uses all CPU cores
  • ⚠️Manual env management — risky at scale
  • ❌No rollback without git history
Setup: SAR 0 · Monthly: SAR 0 · Time: 1 hr
# Install & start npm ci --production pm2 start ecosystem.config.js pm2 save && pm2 startup # Zero-downtime reload pm2 reload all # Cluster mode (use all cores) pm2 start app.js -i max
COMMON
GitHub Actions CI/CD
Automated deploy on git push
  • ✅Push to main → auto deploy to server
  • ✅Run tests before deploy (Jest, lint)
  • ✅Free for public repos, 2000 min/mo free
  • ✅Slack/email notifications on failure
  • ⚠️SSH key management required
Setup: SAR 0 · Monthly: SAR 0–30 · Time: 3–6 hrs
# .github/workflows/deploy.yml on: push: branches: [main] jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - run: npm ci && npm test - name: Deploy uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1 with: host: ${{ secrets.SERVER_IP }} script: | cd /app && git pull docker compose up -d --build
ADVANCED
Kubernetes (K8s)
Multi-region · 500+ merchant scale
  • ✅Auto-scaling: scale pods by CPU/memory
  • ✅Self-healing: restart failed containers
  • ✅Rolling deploys, canary releases
  • ⚠️Overhead: SAR 150/mo minimum (managed)
  • ❌Complex — overkill under 300 merchants
Setup: SAR 0 · Monthly: SAR 150–600 · Time: 20–40 hrs
# Deploy to K8s kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml kubectl rollout status deploy/bss-api # Auto-scale 2–10 replicas kubectl autoscale deploy bss-api \ --cpu-percent=60 --min=2 --max=10 # Rolling update kubectl set image deploy/bss-api \ api=registry/bss-api:v1.3
SERVERLESS
Serverless (Lambda / Cloud Run)
Event-driven · Low/variable traffic
  • ✅Pay per invocation — SAR 0 when idle
  • ✅Zero server management
  • ⚠️Cold start latency 100–500ms
  • ❌DB connections: use PgBouncer/pooler
  • ❌Not ideal for ZATCA long-running jobs
Setup: SAR 0 · Monthly: SAR 0–200 · Time: 4–8 hrs
# AWS Lambda (Node.js) serverless deploy --stage prod # Google Cloud Run gcloud run deploy bss-api \ --image gcr.io/proj/bss-api:v1 \ --region me-central1 \ --min-instances 1 \ --max-instances 20
PLATFORM
